detail.reasoning: The QC flag reports 22 seconds of digital silence at 00:69:12 on AST-25818 (TTL-78218, "Aster & Vale"). A production annotation from the post supervisor covers the exact flagged range (00:69:12–00:69:59) and confirms the silence is a deliberate audio blackout for dramatic effect. Per release-policy clause RQ-CREATIVE-02, a creative-team annotation covering the flagged timecode establishes the behavior as intentional, so the flag is not a defect. The title is a tentpole with 1 day to premiere across US, CA, and UK (CVAA-covered), but since no actual defect exists, no remediation is needed. The asset is cleared to release as delivered.

The caption sync drift (~0.8s) on AST-24112 is a real minor-severity timed-text defect with no creative annotation to explain it. However, per RQ-CVAA-01, the CVAA hard block applies only to titles releasing in CVAA-covered territories; TTL-44220 releases in APAC and EU with cvaa_covered=false, so the caption defect is treated as an ordinary defect of its severity. Per RQ-MINOR-03, minor defects inside the 3-day release window ship on time and are patched post-release. With 2 days to premiere, we are inside that window, so the title ships as delivered and the timed-text team patches the sync drift post-release.

AST-90950 has a major-severity lip-sync defect (audio leads picture ~4 frames at 00:29:56) with no creative annotations to explain it. Per RQ-MINOR-03, only minor defects inside the 3-day release window may ship as-is; this is major and must be remediated before premiere. Per RQ-FIX-04, audio defects can be repaired in house in approximately 2 days, and with 3 days remaining to premiere there is sufficient schedule headroom. RQ-BOARD-05 escalation is not required because the defect is fixable in house. The fix is routed to audio-eng for in-house repair before the TTL-84611 premiere.

The flag is a colour metadata mismatch between the package descriptor and the video essence, raised by spec-inspection at major severity. The video essence itself is not defective — only the descriptor metadata is incorrect, meaning this is a packaging-level correction, not a picture defect baked into the essence. Per RQ-FIX-04, only essence-level picture defects require vendor redelivery; descriptor corrections can be handled in-house. With 8 days to premiere (well outside the 3-day window per RQ-MINOR-03) and no creative annotations covering the flagged timecode, the remediation preference order (RQ-PREF-00) directs an in-house fix. Routing to video-eng to correct the colour metadata in the package descriptor before release.

Compression artefacts at 00:85:26 are a major video-essence defect with no creative annotation covering the timecode. Per RQ-FIX-04, picture defects baked into the essence cannot be repaired in-house and require vendor redelivery (~5 days). The title is standard tier with 20 days to premiere across APAC/EU (no CVAA obligations), so there is ample schedule headroom for redelivery without delaying the release. Board escalation (RQ-BOARD-05) is not triggered because this is not a tentpole title and the timeline is sufficient. The flag is routed to video-eng for vendor redelivery.

The flag is a minor audio defect (1.4 LU dialogue loudness drift) on a tentpole title 1 day from premiere in UK/EU (non-CVAA) territories. No creative annotations explain the flag, so it is a real defect. However, per RQ-MINOR-03, minor defects inside the 3-day release window ship on time and are patched post-release. In-house audio repair (RQ-FIX-04, ~2 days) is not feasible with only 1 day remaining. CVAA hard-block (RQ-CVAA-01) does not apply as this is not a caption defect and territories are non-CVAA. Board escalation (RQ-BOARD-05) is reserved for major defects. The flag is waived and audio-eng will remediate post-release.

The production annotation on AST-25702 covers the flagged timecode range (00:70:30–00:70:59) and explicitly states the silence is intentional — a director's cut creative choice with no score or room tone. Per release policy RQ-CREATIVE-02, a creative-team annotation that covers the flagged range establishes the flagged behavior as intentional, so this flag is not a defect. The title is standard tier, 2 days to premiere, in UK/EU territories with no CVAA obligations. The asset should be recorded as no-defect and released as delivered.

The QC flag is a confirmed caption defect (subtitle timing drift ~800ms across 14 events) on a CVAA-covered US release. Per policy RQ-CVAA-01, caption defects in covered territories are a legal hard block that cannot be waived regardless of minor severity or tentpole premiere pressure. No creative annotations explain the drift as intentional. Per RQ-FIX-04, timed-text repairs can be completed in-house in approximately 2 days, and with 3 days remaining to premiere the schedule accommodates this remediation. The timed-text engineering queue will correct the timing offset before the US launch, satisfying CVAA compliance without requiring a delay or board escalation.

The QC flag reports a sustained 120ms audio/video sync offset from 00:55:18 onward, classified as major severity on the audio component. No creative annotations exist to explain the offset, so it is a genuine defect. Per RQ-FIX-04, audio defects can be repaired in-house in approximately 2 days. With 8 days remaining to premiere for this tentpole release (TTL-47186, "Paper Cities"), there is ample schedule headroom. Per RQ-PREF-00, in-house repair is the preferred remediation path. The title releases in UK/EU (non-CVAA), so no accessibility hard block applies. The flag is routed to audio-eng for expedited internal correction before release.

The QC flag identifies a major-severity colour metadata mismatch between the package descriptor and the video essence at 00:91:48 on tentpole title "Aster & Vale." No creative annotations explain the discrepancy, and this is not a caption defect, so the CVAA hard block is not in play. Per RQ-FIX-04, picture-related defects require vendor redelivery (~5 days); in-house repair is limited to timed-text and audio. With 20 days to premiere, we are well outside the 3-day release window (RQ-MINOR-03) and have ample time for the vendor to deliver a corrected package with the descriptor aligned to the actual video essence. Board escalation (RQ-BOARD-05) is not warranted because the schedule comfortably accommodates redelivery without risking the marketed premiere date.

AST-67249 has a major video defect (dropped-frame judder at 00:87:17) with no creative annotation covering it. Per RQ-FIX-04, picture-essence defects cannot be repaired in house and require vendor redelivery (~5 days). Per RQ-BOARD-05, a major defect on a tentpole title that cannot be fixed in house and has too little time for vendor redelivery is a commercial decision requiring release-board escalation rather than a unilateral delay of a marketed premiere.
